theme: adaptive materials assay selecting which sample to test
title: The Aliased Assay: Which Sample to Test
innovation_hook: Choose assays for their power to break the theta-variance aliasing between surviving hypotheses, not for marginal information under the current posterior.
strategies: do nothing: run no assay; report the prior-weighted mean of theta (reproduces the evaluator baseline, ~0.1)

information-per-cost greedy: myopically buy the affordable assay with the largest identification-entropy reduction per lab-hour until the budget is spent -- the textbook adaptive-experiment recipe, which follows prior-weight and ignores theta spread, so it breaks the wrong (high-weight, low-variance) alias

variance-targeted design + local search: optimise the true objective (within-class weighted theta-variance + gamma*cost) directly by greedy addition then budget-respecting remove/add/swap local search, spending the budget on the theta-extreme aliases that dominate the estimation error

stronger search: knapsack/ILP over assay subsets, submodular-with-cost lazy greedy on the variance objective, beam search or simulated annealing over designs, or an adaptive (sequential) decision policy
